name: Camino Mozarabe a Santiago (Cañaveral - Galisteo) — round trip to the Embalse del Boquerón
name_original: Camino Mozarabe a Santiago (Cañaveral - Galisteo) — iday vuelta por el Embalse del Boquerón
region_intro: Jogging around Riolobos offers a diverse range of routes through varied terrain. The region features open dehesa landscapes, paths alongside the Canal del Río Alagón, and routes with gentle elevation changes. Runners can explore a mix of flat sections and moderate ascents, providing options for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Riolobos The most popular jogging route is Views of Galisteo — circular by the Dehesa de S. Gil, an easy 2.9 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 30 minutes to complete. This route offers views across the Dehesa de S. Gil. Another top favourite among local runners is Landscapes of Galisteo — circular, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This route explores the varied landscapes of Galisteo, taking approximately 1 hour 7 minutes. Local runners also love the Canal del Río Alagón by the Ermita de Nuestra Señora de La Argamasa — circular from Riolobos, an 8.7 miles (14.1 km) trail leading along the Canal del Río Alagón, often completed in about 1 hour 31 minutes. Jogging around Riolobos is defined by open dehesa, river canals, and routes passing by historical points of interest. The network offers options for easy jogging routes and more challenging running trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Riolobos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Riolobos?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Riolobos, offering a variety of distances and difficulties for all types of runners. These routes are curated to showcase the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Riolobos?
A: Yes, Riolobos offers several easy running options perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. One excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1296735167/views-of-galisteo-circular-by-the-dehesa-de-s-gil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Galisteo — circular by the Dehesa de S. Gil&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.9-mile (4.8 km) trail that takes about 30 minutes to complete.

Q: What is the typical length of running trails around Riolobos?
A: Running trails in Riolobos vary significantly in length. You can find shorter routes like the 2.9-mile (4.8 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1296735167/views-of-galisteo-circular-by-the-dehesa-de-s-gil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Galisteo — circular by the Dehesa de S. Gil&lt;/a&gt;, up to longer options such as the 8.7-mile (14.1 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295171231/canal-del-rio-alagon-by-the-ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-argamasa-circular-from-riolobos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal del Río Alagón by the Ermita de Nuestra Señora de La Argamasa — circular from Riolobos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular running routes in the Riolobos area?
A: Absolutely! Many of the running paths around Riolobos are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1296724982/landscapes-of-galisteo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landscapes of Galisteo — circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295171231/canal-del-rio-alagon-by-the-ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-argamasa-circular-from-riolobos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal del Río Alagón by the Ermita de Nuestra Señora de La Argamasa — circular from Riolobos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails near Riolobos?
A: The running trails around Riolobos offer diverse scenery, from open &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscapes with cork oaks to paths alongside the Canal del Río Alagón. You'll also find routes with gentle elevation changes and views of historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1296735167/views-of-galisteo-circular-by-the-dehesa-de-s-gil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Galisteo — circular by the Dehesa de S. Gil&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic vistas across the Dehesa de S. Gil.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the running routes?
A: Yes, several running routes pass by interesting historical landmarks. You can explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/655984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galisteo City Walls&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galisteo Historic Town and Stone Archway&lt;/a&gt;, or even catch glimpses of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3163244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirabel Castle&lt;/a&gt; on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298253991/mirabel-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirabel Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Riolobos?
A: The best time for running in Riolobos is typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be quite hot, making early mornings or late evenings preferable. Winters are generally mild, offering pleasant conditions for running, especially on sunny days.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running routes in Riolobos?
A: Most outdoor trails in the Riolobos region are generally dog-friendly, provided your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock. Always ensure you carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Riolobos offers routes with more significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991211828/umbria-del-barbechoso-running-circular-from-mirabel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Umbría del Barbechoso – running circular from Mirabel&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 6.9-mile (11.1 km) route with over 300 meters of ascent, providing a good workout.

Q: Are the running routes around Riolobos suitable for families?
A: Yes, many of the easier and moderate routes are suitable for families, especially those with older children. The relatively flat sections and well-defined paths make for an enjoyable experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1296735167/views-of-galisteo-circular-by-the-dehesa-de-s-gil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Galisteo — circular by the Dehesa de S. Gil&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for a family outing.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Riolobos?
A: Runners often praise the tranquility and natural beauty of the Riolobos area. The diverse landscapes, from open &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; to riverside paths, provide a refreshing escape. The komoot community has actively explored these routes, with over 50 visitors sharing their experiences.

Q: Is public transport available to access the running trails?
A: While Riolobos is a smaller community, local bus services might connect to nearby towns. However, for direct access to many trailheads, especially those outside the main village, personal transport is generally recommended. Parking is often available near starting points in villages or designated areas.
tips: Small reservoir that is part of the Arroyo del Rivero and that fills this small dam near the town of Riolobos. The area is called Dehesa la Torrecilla del Carmen.
An old path that was already used in Mozarabic times and that runs through these precise landscapes of Dehesa typical of this area of the peninsula.
